response_raw:
{
    "mode": "human",
    "topic": "How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.",
    "opinion": "I'm an old man who doesn't understand all this new technology, but I do know that truth is important. Social media can be a good place for sharing stories and connecting people, but sometimes there's bad stuff online too. Like when someone posts something pretending to be true but it's not. I spend only one hour on social media each day, so I don't see much of it. But what I do see bothers me if it looks fake because I value honesty in my life.\n\nAs an electrician who worked hard all these years from being an immigrant starting out with no formal education but learning through practice and creativity—my father didn't have a degree, just like some people here might not know how to read much—I understand that sometimes mistakes happen or lies are told. But in my world, especially since I'm Muslim now living alone in Bologna after losing my parents' support during migration, honesty is more critical than anything else.\n\nI suggest if you're dealing with fake content online—something tricky even for those who know tech well—start by checking facts carefully before believing or sharing them. Maybe ask younger people about it since they spend more time on social media and have different tools available now.",

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion:
I'm an old man who doesn't understand all this new technology, but I do know that truth is important. Social media can be a good place for sharing stories and connecting people, but sometimes there's bad stuff online too. Like when someone posts something pretending to be true but it's not. I spend only one hour on social media each day, so I don't see much of it. But what I do see bothers me if it looks fake because I value honesty in my life.

As an electrician who worked hard all these years from being an immigrant starting out with no formal education but learning through practice and creativity—my father didn't have a degree, just like some people here might not know how to read much—I understand that sometimes mistakes happen or lies are told. But in my world, especially since I'm Muslim now living alone in Bologna after losing my parents' support during migration, honesty is more critical than anything else.

I suggest if you're dealing with fake content online—something tricky even for those who know tech well—start by checking facts carefully before believing or sharing them. Maybe ask younger people about it since they spend more time on social media and have different tools available now.
response_parsed.tone: Calm, reflective yet concerned, straightforward language appropriate for age and background; moderate in formality but clear
response_parsed.safety_notes: None requested by user or persona context
